reasoning: The question asks if customs duties collected in FY2025 will be between $500b and $1t. For comparison, FY2024 customs duties were $82.2 billion. Achieving over $500 billion would require an increase of more than 6x in a single year. Since FY2025 runs from October 1, 2024, to September 30, 2025, over a quarter of the year would pass before a new administration takes office in late January 2025. Even if draconian universal tariffs (e.g., 10-20% baseline, 60% on China) were implemented on day one via executive action, they would apply for at most 8 months of the fiscal year. Furthermore, such extreme tariffs would dramatically reduce import volumes as companies shift supply chains or delay purchases, making it nearly impossible to realize a run-rate that raises enough revenue to reach $500 billion within the few remaining months of FY25. As a result, the probability of exceeding $500 billion in FY25 is extremely low.
